page: Microsoft Azure DC32s_v3 is a dedicated virtual machine instance from the DCSv3 family, built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The processor includes a 48 MB L3 cache and supports Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and cryptographic acceleration via AES and VAES. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, relying instead on external Premium Storage.

In benchmark evaluations, the DC32s_v3 demonstrates strong multi-core processing capabilities. It achieves high scores in multi-core CPU stress tests, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, and Passmark CPU encryption and extended instructions tests. Database operations and static web serving throughput also rank in the top tier. However, single-core performance and memory bandwidth remain in the average range. While the instance handles prompt processing for smaller language models efficiently, text generation for large 70B models is a notable bottleneck, registering in the bottom performance tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the DC32s_v3 offers a dense allocation of dedicated physical cores and substantial memory without the added cost of bundled local storage or GPU hardware. This makes it a balanced choice for memory-intensive workloads that do not require local NVMe drives. The instance is well-suited for database operations, multi-threaded data processing, static web serving, and enterprise applications running on Microsoft Hyper-V.
description: Microsoft Azure DC32s_v3 is a dedicated x86_64 virtual machine from the DCSv3 family, featuring 32 Intel Xeon 8370C physical cores running at 2.8 GHz and 256.0 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPUs, this instance relies on external Premium Storage. It delivers strong multi-core performance, as evidenced by high marks in multi-threaded decompression, database operations, and cryptographic benchmarks. While single-core speeds and memory bandwidth are average, its high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core makes it highly efficient for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. The server is optimal for database hosting, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing, though it is not suited for large-scale LLM text generation.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DC32s_v3 is a dedicated virtual machine featuring 32 Intel Xeon cores, 256 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering strong multi-core performance for databases and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DC32s_v3 is a dedicated 32-core Intel Xeon VM with 256 GB RAM, optimized for databases and multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
tagline: A dedicated multi-core Intel processor instance with high memory density, optimized for enterprise databases and secure multi-threaded workloads.

256.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Strong multi-core CPU, database, and cryptographic benchmark performance
No local storage, requiring external Premium Storage solutions
